Title: Post-transcriptional and post-translational regulation of anthocyanin biosynthesis in sweetpotato by Ib-miR2111 and IbKFB: Implications for health promotion

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Putative modulator associated with anthocyanin regulation through interaction with IbKFB; direct causal effect on anthocyanin not functionally tested
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Studied from tuberous-root cDNA library; specific expression pattern not reported
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Small Auxin Up RNA (SAUR) family protein
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Y2H library screening from tuberous roots; transcriptome context available (accession NA)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was experimentally validated as an IbKFB interactor in results, but no direct degradation or transgenic phenotyping for anthocyanin was shown
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May affect growth and stress responses based on SAUR family roles
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary candidate for future study rather than immediate biofortification target
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IbSAUR50 was identified from IbKFB interaction screening. 2. Interaction with IbKFB was validated by Y2H and BiFC. 3. Its specific contribution to anthocyanin accumulation remains unresolved in this study.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seedlings; main stem and rosette leaf junctions
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of anthocyanin accumulation in Arabidopsis; Atkfb mutant displays increased purple pigmentation and anthocyanin
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced in Ib-miR2111-OE and Atkfb plants; increased in STTM-At-miR2111 and IbKFB-OE lines
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant analysis; heterologous Ib-miR2111 overexpression; STTM inhibition; anthocyanin quantification; qRT-PCR
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as Arabidopsis functional ortholog/control to support conservation of the miR2111-KFB module
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports cross-species conservation of KFB editing as a strategy to enhance anthocyanin
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Atkfb mutant accumulates markedly more anthocyanin. 2. Ib-miR2111 overexpression in Arabidopsis suppresses KFB expression and increases anthocyanin biosynthetic gene expression. 3. Arabidopsis data support conserved negative regulation by KFB proteins.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylpropanoid pathway leading to anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step supplying the anthocyanin branch of phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid, initiating phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream; committed entry into phenylpropanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive contributor to anthocyanin biosynthesis; its degradation by IbKFB reduces anthocyanin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Inferred to be functionally important in sweetpotato tuberous roots where anthocyanin accumulates; protein studied from tuberous-root cDNA library
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; ubiquitination assay; cited Co-IP-related evidence for ubiquitination substrate status
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key upstream control point, but rate-limiting status not directly established in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome; small RNA; degradome sequencing from sweetpotato tuberous roots (accession NA)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as an IbKFB interactor and ubiquitination substrate; paper emphasizes post-translational destabilization rather than direct transgenic manipulation of IbPAL1
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Degradation could broadly reduce phenylpropanoid-derived compounds beyond anthocyanin
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful stacking target to avoid IbKFB-mediated degradation and enhance anthocyanin flux
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IbPAL1 physically interacts with IbKFB. 2. IbKFB mediates ubiquitination and degradation of IbPAL1. 3. Destabilization of IbPAL1 helps explain reduced anthocyanin biosynthesis when IbKFB is abundant.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Primary carbon metabolism linked to phenylpropanoid/anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Precursor/energy supply module supporting anthocyanin production
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidation and phosphorylation of glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate in glycolysis, supporting metabolic energy/intermediate supply
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream support metabolism outside core anthocyanin branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor/energy pool supporting target metabolite biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive supporting factor for anthocyanin accumulation; IbKFB-mediated degradation is associated with reduced anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Studied from sweetpotato tuberous-root cDNA and interaction assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; Co-IP; GST pull-down; ubiquitination assay
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome; small RNA; degradome sequencing from sweetpotato tuberous roots (accession NA)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a canonical anthocyanin pathway enzyme; paper proposes it affects anthocyanin through energy/metabolic intermediate supply and is a direct ubiquitination substrate of IbKFB
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulating this gene may affect central carbon metabolism and broader growth/energy traits
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary engineering target, but caution is needed because it participates in primary metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IbGAPCp1 physically interacts with IbKFB in multiple assays. 2. IbKFB promotes ubiquitination of IbGAPCp1. 3. Destabilization of IbGAPCp1 may reduce metabolic support for anthocyanin biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: XS-18 (isolated for cloning); naturally higher in purple-fleshed lines including XZS-3/XZS-8/NZS-4
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: XS-18; comparative varieties XZS-3, XZS-8, NZS-4, SL-1, SS-8
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Tuberous roots (flesh and skin); also pigmentation in callus and plantlets
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis via repression of a negative regulator
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses IbKFB, thereby indirectly increasing expression of IbCHS; IbCHI; IbDFR; IbANS
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong positive regulator; overexpression markedly increases anthocyanin accumulation in sweetpotato and Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation; Ib-miR2111-OE sweetpotato roots showed 5.8-fold higher anthocyanin than WT, while CRISPR-Ib-miR2111 reduced anthocyanin
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in purple-fleshed sweetpotato varieties than white-fleshed varieties; Ib-miR2111a expressed higher than Ib-miR2111b
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in anthocyanin-rich tuberous roots; increased in Ib-miR2111-OE and reduced in CRISPR-Ib-miR2111 lines
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Small RNA-seq; transcriptome; degradome sequencing from tuberous roots (accession NA)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Degradome sequencing; psRNATarget prediction; tobacco transient co-expression; overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 editing; northern blotting; metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Paper suggests possible broader roles in stress responses and development based on known miR2111 biology; specific penalties in sweetpotato not established
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising biofortification target to increase root anthocyanin and associated health benefits; could be manipulated by overexpression or release from inhibitory lncRNA networks
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Overexpression construct pC2300-pOT2-Ib-pri-MIR2111; promoter type not explicitly stated
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ib-miR2111 is preferentially expressed in purple-fleshed sweetpotato tuberous roots. 2. It directly cleaves IbKFB transcripts and suppresses IbKFB expression. 3. Overexpression enhances anthocyanin accumulation and upregulates anthocyanin biosynthetic genes, while CRISPR disruption lowers anthocyanin. 4. Anthocyanin-rich extracts from Ib-miR2111-OE sweetpotato showed stronger anti-inflammatory and pro-apoptotic effects in MH7A cells.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: F-box ubiquitin ligase adaptor / post-translational regulator
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Targets interacting proteins for ubiquitination and 26S proteasome-mediated degradation; negative regulation of pathway flux
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Post-translational control of phenylpropanoid/anthocyanin biosynthesis and precursor-support metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: IbPAL1; IbGAPCp1; IbSAUR50 (interaction validated, degradation not shown for SAUR50)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes degradation of IbPAL1 and IbGAPCp1, leading to reduced anthocyanin biosynthesis and lower expression of IbCHS; IbCHI; IbDFR; IbANS in planta
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely SCF-type E3 ubiquitin ligase machinery via F-box function; exact complex members not directly tested
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong negative regulator of anthocyanin accumulation in sweetpotato tuberous roots
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses anthocyanin accumulation; overexpression reduces anthocyanin, while CRISPR-IbKFB increases anthocyanin 2.9-fold over WT
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in white-fleshed than purple-fleshed sweetpotato varieties; inversely correlated with Ib-miR2111
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Elevated in IbKFB-OE lines and reduced function in CRISPR-IbKFB lines; higher in anthocyanin-poor varieties
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MG132 proteasome inhibitor used to assess substrate ubiquitination
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phylogenetically close to Arabidopsis AtKFB47 (AT3G27150); contains one F-box domain and two Kelch motifs
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Kelch repeat F-box protein with F-box domain and two Kelch motifs
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Small RNA-seq; transcriptome; degradome sequencing from tuberous roots (accession NA)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Degradome-supported targeting by Ib-miR2111; transient co-expression; overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 editing; Y2H; BiFC; Co-IP; GST pull-down; ubiquitination assays; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Because it also interacts with proteins linked to growth and central metabolism, manipulation may influence broader developmental or metabolic traits
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent knockout/editing target for anthocyanin biofortification in sweetpotato; caution warranted for pleiotropic effects
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pC1300-IbKFB-GFP overexpression construct; promoter type not explicitly stated
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IbKFB is a direct target of Ib-miR2111 and shows inverse expression with anthocyanin accumulation. 2. IbKFB acts as a negative regulator of anthocyanin biosynthesis in Arabidopsis and sweetpotato. 3. IbKFB interacts with IbPAL1, IbGAPCp1, and IbSAUR50. 4. It mediates ubiquitination and degradation of IbPAL1 and IbGAPCp1, thereby repressing anthocyanin production.
